concerns.text: The plan cites 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/SimonZh/SGtest/resolve/main/data/final.parquet' as the primary SGTE source. SGTE (Scientific Group Thermodata Europe) data is proprietary and typically not available as a raw, open-access Parquet file on HuggingFace under a generic 'SGtest' repository. This URL likely points to a synthetic or unrelated dataset, not the verified SGTE thermodynamic database required by FR-001. If the dataset lacks the specific experimental phase boundary coordinates (temperature vs. composition) for binary systems, the entire hypothesis testing pipeline fails. A verified, accessible source for SGTE or NIST-JANAF experimental phase data must be identified.

The fallback URL 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/manifoldix/sg_testset_fhnw/resolve/main/data/test-00000-of-00008.parquet' is cited as an alternative. Similar to the primary source, this appears to be a generic test set or a specific benchmark (possibly FHNW) rather than the comprehensive SGTE/NIST-JANAF experimental database. Relying on these unverified HuggingFace links for the core 'experimental ground truth' risks using a dataset that does not contain the necessary thermodynamic variables (liquidus/solidus temperatures) or contains only synthetic data, rendering the ML training invalid.

The plan states 'NIST-JANAF: No verified source found in the input block' and relies on a local fallback CSV. NIST-JANAF is a copyrighted, paid database. The plan does not cite a specific, accessible, and verified open-source alternative (e.g., a specific open repository containing extracted NIST-JANAF data) that satisfies FR-001. If the local CSV is missing or the SGTE HuggingFace links are not the actual SGTE database, the project has no data source to execute the pipeline, violating the 'Verified Accuracy' principle.
